title: ilex obcordata
intro: Ilex obcordata, also known as the Evergreen Huckleberry or Mountain Huckleberry, is a shrub or small tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, characterized by its glossy, dark green leaves and small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Ilex obcordata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various ailments. Ilex obcordata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
